case_id: MV OOCL United Kingdom / Solent Anchorage

pi_items.source: Web search + MarineTraffic (IMO 9776200, real container vessel)
Web search for OOCL United Kingdom specifications; multiple maritime databases confirm 399.8m LOA
Web search results from maritime vessel databases confirm beam of 58.8 meters
Geographic coordinates within Solent anchorage area (UK), confirmed plausible by geographic search
Geographic coordinates within Solent anchorage area (off Hampshire, England); Port Solent reference point is 50.842991, -1.101002
Reasonable depth for Solent anchorage but not verified against specific location data
Valid anchor cable scope but specific to this scenario instance (synthetic)
Web search confirms maritime shackle = 15 fathoms = 90 feet = 27.432 meters; 27.5m is rounded standard approximation
Mathematical verification using Pythagorean theorem: sqrt(247.5² - 22²) = 246.5245... ≈ 246.52 m
Mathematical verification: LOA + horizontal_projected = 399.9 + 246.52 = 646.42 m (matches maritime swing circle calculation formula)
Standard tolerance for maritime verification but specific value not independently verified

privileged_info_summary: The task involves the real container vessel MV OOCL United Kingdom (IMO 9776200, LOA 399.8-399.9m, beam 58.8m). The vessel data and all swing circle calculations are mathematically correct: chain length of 247.5m (9 shackles × 27.5m), horizontal projection of 246.52m (accounting for 22m water depth via sqrt(247.5²-22²)), and resulting swing radius of 646.42m (LOA + horizontal_projection). The Solent anchorage coordinates (50.765°N, 1.08°W) are geographically accurate for the Hampshire/Isle of Wight region. The vessel data is based on a real ship; however, the specific scenario instance is synthetically created for the task.
